rulings.Case Title: JACKSON ROYBAL et al VS. CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDIAL CENTER et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, JULY 23, 2008. LINE 9, DEFENDANT DIANA FARMER, DIANA FARMER, M.D., INC., A CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE ALLEGATIONS OF THE COMPLAINT ARE GOVERNED BY THREE DECISIONS THAT MAKE CLEAR THAT PLAINTIFF CANNOT STATE A CLAIM FOR NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. THENG VS LACHUSA (1989) 48 CAL. 3D 644; BIRD VS SAENZ (2002) 28 CAL. 4TH 910 AND GOLDSTEIN VS SUPERIOR COURT (1990) 223 CAL. APP 3D 1415. PLAINTIFF'S EFFORTS TO PLACE THIS CASE WITHIN THE HOLDING OF OCHVA VS SUPERIOR COURT (1985) 39 CAL 3D 159 FAIL. WITHOUT QUESTION, PLAINTIFFS SUFFERED WATCHING THEIR CHILD SUFFER AND WERE CONCERNED ABOUT THE TREATMENT ON THE ALLEGED LACK OF TREATMENT THE CHILD WAS RECEIVING. THE COMPLAINT FAILS TO ALLEGE FACTS THAT PROVIDE A BASIS FOR CONCLUSION THAT A LAY PERSON COULD PERCEIVE A MISDIAGNOSIS OR FAILURE TO DIAGNOSIS THE PRESENCE OF A SPONGE IN THE CHILD'S ABDOMEN. SEC e.g. MEIGHAN VS. SHORE (1995) 34 CAL. APP. 4TH 1025. GIVEN PLAINTIFF'S LENGTH OF COMPLAINT, THE COURT FAILS TO PERCEIVE WHAT FACTS COULD BE PLEAD TO OVERCOME CONTROLLING CASE LAW. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: JACKSON ROYBAL et al VS. CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDIAL CENTER et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, JULY 23, 2008. LINE 9, DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE ALLEGATIONS OF THE COMPLAINT ARE GOVERNED BY THREE DECISIONS THAT MAKE CLEAR THAT PLAINTIFF CANNOT STATE A CLAIM FOR NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. THENG VS LACHUSA (1989) 48 CAL. 3D 644; BIRD VS SAENZ (2002) 28 CAL. 4TH 910 AND GOLDSTEIN VS SUPERIOR COURT (1990) 223 CAL. APP 3D 1415. PLAINTIFF'S EFFORTS TO PLACE THIS CASE WITHIN THE HOLDING OF OCHVA VS SUPERIOR COURT (1985) 39 CAL 3D 159 FAIL. WITHOUT QUESTION, PLAINTIFFS SUFFERED WATCHING THEIR CHILD SUFFER AND WERE CONCERNED ABOUT THE TREATMENT ON THE ALLEGED LACK OF TREATMENT THE CHILD WAS RECEIVING. THE COMPLAINT FAILS TO ALLEGE FACTS THAT PROVIDE A BASIS FOR CONCLUSION THAT A LAY PERSON COULD PERCEIVE A MISDIAGNOSIS OR FAILURE TO DIAGNOSIS THE PRESENCE OF A SPONGE IN THE CHILD'S ABDOMEN. SEC e.g. MEIGHAN VS. SHORE (1995) 34 CAL. APP. 4TH 1025. GIVEN PLAINTIFF'S LENGTH OF COMPLAINT, THE COURT FAILS TO PERCEIVE WHAT FACTS COULD BE PLEAD TO OVERCOME CONTROLLING CASE LAW. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: SILVIA REYNOSO VS. STATE OF CALIFORNIA OF TRANSPORTATION DBA CALTRANS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Special Motion And Motion To Strike Prusuant To Ccp Section 425.16
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, JULY 23, 2008. LINE 11, DEFENDANT STEPHEN RAFFLE, M.D.'S Notice Of Special Motion And Motion To Strike Pursuant To Ccp Section 425.16. THE MOTION TO STRIKE IS GRANTED. THE CLAIMS ALLEGED CENTER ON PROTECTED, i.e. DR. RAFFLE SERVING AS AN EXPERT WITNESS IN A LITIGATED CASE. BRIGG VS EDEN COUNSEL FOR HOPE NAD OPPORTUNITY (1999) 19 CAL. 4TH 1106, 1116; TAHERI LAW GROUP VS EVANS (2008) 72 CAL RPTR. 847, FOX SEARCHLIGHT PICTURES, INC. VS PALADINO (2001) 89 CAL. APP. 4TH 294. PLAINTIFF HAS OFFERED NO EVIDENCE EXTABLISHING A BASIS FOR PLAINTIFF TO PREVAIL. THIS PARTICULARLY TRUE GIVEN THE BREADTH OF THE LITIGATION PRIVILEGE. THE PRIVILEGE APPLIES TO ALL COMMUNICATIONS MADE IN LITIGATION "WHETHER FRAUDULENT PERJURIORES, UNETHICAL OR EVEN ILLEGAL," KASHIAN VS HARRIMAN (2002) 98 CAL. APP. 4TH 892, 920. =(302/PJM/AY)
